danger_signals.visual_form: Strange crystals not native to environment; creatures transformed by consuming them become visually monstrously powerful
danger_signals.meaning: External destructive force interfering with natural evolution; creatures eating crystals become dangerous boss-tier threats

danger_signals.visual_form: Significantly larger and more visually distinct creatures at end of levels
danger_signals.meaning: Evolved crystal-corrupted lifeforms that block progression; much stronger than typical enemies

danger_signals.visual_form: Upper UI bar showing player health decreasing during combat
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate danger to player character; loss of all health triggers revival penalty

danger_signals.visual_form: Revealed to be gigantic single-celled organism with advanced crystalline evolution
danger_signals.meaning: Ultimate threat - controlling all lifeforms across planet; final antagonistic force blocking Eden access

safety_signals.visual_form: Divine character guiding player through geological periods; mystical benevolent embodiment
safety_signals.meaning: Protective force ensuring proper evolution flow; player character is favored by Earth's nurturing entity

safety_signals.visual_form: Meat from defeated enemies; nearby plant life in environments
safety_signals.meaning: Health restoration available; environment supports survival and recovery

safety_signals.visual_form: Crystals that display hints/tips or grant large evolution points; temporary unique body transformations
safety_signals.meaning: Benevolent power-ups supporting player advancement; divine assistance in progression

safety_signals.visual_form: Gaia revives player character after health loss; return to map screen
safety_signals.meaning: No permanent failure state; Gaia's protective intervention allows continued play (with evolution point penalty)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Divine embodiment of Earth; Sol's daughter
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appears across all five geological periods as guiding presence; presence in crystals granting benevolent power-ups

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Creatures native to each geological era without crystal corruption
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Standard enemies defeated for evolution points; vary by era (aquatic in fish age, dinosaurs in mesozoic, mammals in ice age)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Common throughout levels; must be confronted by biting, ramming, jumping mechanics

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Strange non-native crystals; visually monstrously transformed creatures
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Boss-tier lifeforms with dramatically altered appearances; crystal-enhanced physiology
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appears across each age as dominant threat; introduced by advanced Martian civilization; disrupts resource flow and evolution trajectory

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Gigantic single-celled organism evolved via crystal consumption; falsely believes itself first human
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Most visually distinct and powerful entity; controls other lifeforms through mysterious external force
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Final Age adversary; controls Eden access; primary narrative antagonist in Age of Eden

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Aquatic environments; water-based level design; player character designed with aquatic body adaptations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy creatures are aquatic species; biting and ramming combat focused; navigation emphasizes swimming mechanics

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Carboniferous Period - Early Creatures of Land
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Transitional terrestrial environments; early land creature physiology available for evolution
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Shift from purely aquatic to amphibian and early terrestrial behaviors; new movement abilities unlock

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Prehistoric landscape; dinosaur-form body parts available for evolution; large reptilian creatures dominate visually
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Dinosaur enemies encountered; horn ramming and tail swing abilities become viable; boss encounters feature dramatically sized creatures

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cold environment aesthetics; mammalian body structures available; visual reminder of dinosaur remnants in final era
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Early mammal hybrid forms; lingering dinosaur encounters; increased evolution complexity with mammalian physiology

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Early Quaternary Period - Age of Man / Age of Eden
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Humanoid evolution available; garden/paradise realm visual design for Eden location; Bolbox's true form revealed as cosmic entity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Final confrontation with crystal-corrupted antagonist; player obtains intelligence gift upon victory; completion state signals evolutionary triumph

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increased visual distinction of corrupted creatures; monstrously transformed boss forms; strange non-native crystals visible in environments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Corrupted lifeforms dominate resource zones; increased difficulty spikes per era; creatures visibly enhanced beyond natural state

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Health bar UI depletes; character may show visual damage states during combat
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player forced to seek meat/plant consumption; evasion becomes priority; revival penalty (50% evolution point loss) threatened

environmental_storytelling.technique: Geological era progression as visual narrative
environmental_storytelling.example: Five distinct map screens representing 500+ million years of Earth history; visual design shifts from aquatic to terrestrial to mammalian to human, telling evolutionary story through level geography
Strange non-native crystals appearing across environments signal Martian interference; their presence visually corrupts and transforms native creatures into boss-tier threats
Each geological period displays era-appropriate creatures; dinosaurs dominate Mesozoic visually, early mammals appear in Ice Age, signaling time passage through fauna design

environmental_storytelling.technique: Boss visual distinctiveness as narrative emphasis
environmental_storytelling.example: Crystal-corrupted bosses are dramatically more visually impressive than standard enemies, communicating their status as period-defining threats and Martian-influenced antagonists

environmental_storytelling.technique: Body part evolution as character development
environmental_storytelling.example: Eight customizable body sections that visually change appearance when upgraded; player's creature becomes more powerful and complex-looking as evolution progresses, reflecting literal transformation narrative

Gaia's benevolent crystals contrast visually with corrupting Martian crystals; revival mechanic shows Gaia intervention through respawn at map screen rather than game-over, communicating her protective presence

environmental_storytelling.technique: Final form revelation as climactic visual
environmental_storytelling.example: Bolbox's true identity as gigantic single-celled organism provides shocking visual contrast to its perceived humanoid form; reveals narrative deception through monster design

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Roam levels; must be confronted by biting, ramming with horns, jumping on them, or jumping over them to avoid
Leave behind meat for player consumption; meat grants evolution points for body part upgrades
Significantly stronger than typical enemies; require much more effort to defeat; visually monstrously transformed
Health bar depletes; can restore via consuming enemy meat, eating plant life, or undergoing selective evolution
Revived by Gaia; sent back to map screen; loses approximately 50% of accumulated evolution points as penalty
Sends other lifeforms against player in mysterious external control; blocks Eden access; serves as ultimate antagonistic force
Guides player through geological periods; revives player after defeat; grants divine crystals with tips and power-ups; determines Eden access criteria

notes: E.V.O. uses evolutionary progression as both mechanical system and narrative world-state indicator. Visual danger is communicated primarily through crystal corruption (non-native element), boss scale contrast, and health UI feedback. Safety/benevolence is signaled through Gaia's divine presence, consumable resources, and revival mechanics. The game's faction structure is minimal (no traditional colored factions)—instead, world-state is defined by geological era and degree of crystal corruption. Environmental storytelling relies heavily on era-appropriate creature design, body part evolution visibility, and crystal visual contamination as indicators of Martian interference disrupting natural selection. Regional differences (Japanese text removal, steaming piles deletion in NA version) suggest localization but don't affect core manifestation patterns. Debug mode allows skipping areas and freezing enemy behavior, indicating developer tools for testing world-state transitions. The game's core design philosophy is that visual appearance and behavior of creatures directly reflect the world's evolutionary state and external threat level.
sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - E.V.O.: Search for Eden)
